rules.description: Produce the most natural-sounding translation, capturing intended meaning and transferring it into natural language suited to a wide Traditional Chinese-speaking audience
rules.notes: Avoid literal transposition of English syntax; sentences need to be rewritten with adapted syntax

rules.description: Balance faithfulness to the source and readability in the target language
Use a clear and precise style that is easily understood and does not read like a translation
rules.notes: Put yourself in the reader's place and determine whether the output is clear and fluent, as well as consistent and accurate

rules.description: Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions and Anglicism
rules.notes: Also avoid enriching the text excessively

rules.description: Buttons should typically be translated as verbs, generally using the imperative form
rules.notes: Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions and go straight to the point

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions including terminology lists before translating product, application, third party or feature names
rules.notes: Many product/application names are trademarked and not localizable. Terms marked as DNT (Do not translate) should not be localized

rules.description: Replace English examples by Traditional Chinese equivalents when needed for localization/adaptation
rules.notes: The explanation about the use of wildcards is a good example of text in need of localization/adaptation

rules.description: Be precise and clear, consistent and natural
rules.notes: Rephrase sentences that are not clear enough, and rework sentences that sound like a translation

rules.description: Break up long English sentences for better readability
Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations
Use clear and coherent style so target reader understands the text easily
Be objective: neutral, do not insert your own opinion, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct
Ensure content and numbers are free of mistakes
Use idiomatically correct verbs, metaphors, expressions, etc. so that text does not sound like a translation
Be complete: do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)
Keep an eye on the target reader: use an appropriate tone
Cross-references shall be referenced consistently throughout your translation as well as throughout all files
For external cross-references, check whether this text or document has been localized before and match the relevant translations
rules.notes: Unless otherwise specified by project-specific instructions

rules.description: If unable to retrieve cross-reference information, contact the Project Management team or Language Lead
rules.notes: If unable to receive response in time, leave the title in source language and place proposed translation in parenthesis

rules.description: If the document has not been translated before, leave as in source language
The style should be formal and polite appropriate for business communication
rules.notes: Casual expressions should be avoided

rules.description: Always use 您 instead of 你
rules.example_correct: 請輸入您的使用者名稱

rules.description: Use 您已完成 instead of 您已完成了 for completed actions
rules.example_correct: 您已完成 RingCentral 企業電話系統設定
rules.example_incorrect: 您已完成了 RingCentral 企業電話系統設定

rules.description: Traditional Chinese usually gives priority to masculine forms, unless differently needed based on a specific context
rules.example_correct: 服務團隊會聯絡客戶並告知他
rules.example_incorrect: 服務團隊會聯絡客戶並告知他/她

rules.description: Avoid the passive voice whenever possible, use the active form to improve readability
rules.example_correct: 該主題提供所有詳細資料
rules.example_incorrect: 所有詳細資料都已在此主題提供
rules.notes: Passive voice is only advised in specific texts like legal and official documents

rules.description: Do not transfer the extensive use of English possessive adjectives to target
rules.example_correct: 您可以在電腦或行動裝置上編輯文件
rules.example_incorrect: 您可以在您的電腦，或您的移動設備上編輯您的文件

rules.description: The subject word does not always need to be translated in Traditional Chinese, especially when the subject word is "you"
rules.notes: Omit "you" or replace it with a specific noun

rules.description: "We" should be substituted with the specific noun, such as a company name
For most cases, there is no need to translate articles into Traditional Chinese for fluency and conciseness
rules.notes: But if it is important to emphasize the noun so that the reader does not get confused, then it is suggested to translate the article

rules.description: The -ing form can be translated in different ways based on context, such as a verb, a noun, an infinitive clause, etc.
rules.example_correct: 我們的控制器隨附快取，用於保護資料完整性
rules.notes: Use the most appropriate option based on context

rules.description: Check the context, concentrate on the meaning and render it as clearly as you can
rules.example_correct: 您的姓名、電子郵件位址、公司名稱
rules.example_incorrect: 您的姓名、您的電子郵件位址、您的公司名稱
rules.notes: English sometimes uses additional words to express concepts

rules.description: Use 這意味著 instead of 這代表 for "This means"
rules.example_correct: 這意味著現在可…

rules.description: Repetitions are acceptable in English but might not be in Traditional Chinese
rules.example_correct: 要從電腦中刪除文件，請在右側窗格選擇，然後按一下「刪除」
rules.example_incorrect: 要從電腦删除文件，請在右側窗格選擇文件，然後按一下「删除」
rules.notes: Do not hesitate to use pronouns or other "tricks" to avoid repetitions

rules.description: Brand name tags need to be separated from the preceding or following text with a space
In Traditional Chinese, there is no space before any punctuation sign
rules.notes: However, there are exceptions specified in following sections

rules.description: Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces in Traditional Chinese
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces after full stops

rules.description: Use non-breaking spaces to prevent certain expressions from being separated at the end of a line
rules.notes: Such as people's names, numbers, software names and versions, etc. Use Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or Alt+0160

rules.description: Half-width space needed between Chinese characters and English letters or numbers
rules.example_correct: 可在產品底部找到序號，格式為 XXX1234-5678
rules.example_incorrect: 可在產品底部找到序號，格式為XXX1234-5678

rules.description: Half-width space needed between Chinese characters and half-width punctuations: ( ), [ ]
Use the Chinese comma (，) in both software and document localization
When enumerating several items in English, use the pause symbol (、) instead of (，)
When translating "and" (和) or "or" (或), you can omit the previous comma in Traditional Chinese
A Chinese period is used to end a sentence, a paragraph or an abbreviated word
When a sentence is placed between brackets or quotes, the period is placed outside
Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in a table, or after captions used with illustrations, tables etc.
rules.notes: Unless the expression considered is a full sentence

rules.description: Do not use full stops if a sentence ends with an interrogation or exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Half-width colon (:) is used for software, while Full-width colon (：) is used in documentation
rules.notes: Try to avoid using colons

rules.description: Colon is used when a list of actions or object is following
rules.example_correct:
如需這些主題的進一步資訊：
• 登入
• 變更密碼
• 通話處理
• 緊急電話撥打 – 登錄地點
• 語音信箱問候語

rules.description: Half-width semi colon (;) is used for software, while Full-width semi colon (；) is used in documentation
Semi-colons are used less in Traditional Chinese than in English. Usually Chinese comma (，) is used to replace semi colon in translation
Half-width question mark (?) is used for software, while Full-width question mark (？) is used in documentation
Half-width exclamation mark (!) is used for software, while Full-width exclamation mark (！) is used in documentation
Exclamation marks are not as common in Traditional Chinese as they are in English. Sometimes it is better to remove them
rules.example_correct: 地址無效！
rules.notes: When they appear in groups (!!!), you may keep only one

rules.description: The en dash (–) is used as a minus sign, usually with spaces before and after. In Traditional Chinese, keep it as same as English
The en dash is used in number ranges without spaces around the en dash
rules.notes: Such as those specifying page numbers

rules.description: There are cases when you can omit the en dash
rules.example_correct: 同業佼佼者
rules.notes: For example: best-in-class → 同業佼佼者, 2-factor authentication → 雙因素驗證, all-inclusive → 全包

rules.description: The em dash (—) should not be used in Traditional Chinese as it looks the same as the Traditional Chinese character for "one" (一)
rules.notes: Oftentimes, there is no need for such punctuation through rephrasing. If punctuation is needed, you may use ( ), : or en dash to emphasize the element

rules.description: Use half width round brackets ( ) when the texts included are all English or digits
rules.notes: Spaces should be left before opening round bracket and after ending round bracket

rules.description: Use full width round brackets（）when the texts included are containing Chinese characters
rules.notes: Extra spaces are not needed before and after the full width brackets

rules.description: Always use half width square brackets [ ]
rules.notes: When using square brackets, please leave one space before opening square brackets and one space after ending square brackets

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
rules.notes: Updated on 20241207

rules.description: For UI, in Traditional Chinese, UI should be enclosed with [ ] and remove bold font (if used)
rules.example_correct: 按一下 [同步選項]

rules.description: For titles of user guides, chapters and sections, use《》for books, manuals, etc. Use ＜＞ for chapters and sections
rules.example_correct: 請參閱〈授權應用程式〉一節

rules.description: Use《》for books and manuals
rules.example_correct: 請參閱《RingCentral Office® 使用者指南》

rules.description: Replace quotes (except used for manual, chapter, section names or UI) with square quotes
rules.example_correct: 橘色代表「請勿接受佇列通話」
rules.example_incorrect: 橘色代表"請勿接受佇列通話"

rules.description: Use straight apostrophe consistently in the translations, instead of curly apostrophe

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If the bulleted items are complete sentences (with a conjugated verb), each ends with a period
rules.example_correct:
• 請輸入其他提示名稱，然後重新上傳。
• 請輸入其他提示名稱。
rules.example_incorrect:
• 請輸入其他提示名稱，然後重新上傳
• 請輸入其他提示名稱

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If the bulleted items are phrases or single words, items shall not be followed by a period
rules.example_correct:
• 美國
• 英國
• 加拿大
rules.example_incorrect:
• 美國，
• 英國，
• 加拿大。

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists
rules.description: If the list items are not full sentences and depend syntactically on the introductory sentence, each element should end with a Chinese semicolon (；)
Abbreviations should be avoided whenever possible
rules.notes: But you may need to abbreviate words in item such as UI options

rules.description: Only use commonly used abbreviates, never create any new
On first use, spelling out or translation in brackets followed by the abbreviations
Do not use abbreviations in titles unless you are sure that your intended audience will recognize them
Translation of acronyms depends on RingCentral project-related requirements and should always be investigated to confirm their localizability
If an acronym is used in a title, do not write out the acronym meaning in full
If a translation is possible, keep the English acronym
rules.example_correct: 高性能文件系统 (HPFS)
rules.notes: The expansion comes before its acronym

rules.description: If a translation is not possible, leave expansion and its acronym in parenthesis
rules.example_correct: Desktop Publishing (DTP)

rules.description: The acronym is not followed by its expansion when used alone
rules.example_correct: OLE
rules.notes: Use the acronym without expansion

rules.description: Some acronyms are localizable: AM → 上午, ID → 識別碼, PM → 下午
rules.example_correct: 上午

rules.description: Numbers should be written in full when used as nouns
Numbers should be written in full when at the beginning of a sentence
Numbers should be written in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually the numbers up to 9
Numbers should be written in full in expressions like "in the 80s"
Dates, large numbers, ages, document references (paragraph, page, etc.) should be written as numbers
Replace the decimal point by a comma and the thousand separator by a dot
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Units of measure should be separated from the preceding number by a non-breaking space
rules.example_correct: 533 MHz

rules.description: If content includes units of measures that do not apply to your locale, you might be instructed to add converted value or convert to metric equivalent based on project requirements
rules.notes: Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches and represent one of the few exceptions

rules.description: Percentages are usually written in figures followed by the symbol %
rules.notes: No blank spaces are used between the figure and the symbol

rules.description: Adapt date and time formats to Traditional Chinese standards
Date format should follow month/day. The short format can be separated either by hyphen (-) or slash
Short date format: YYYY/MM/DD
Long date format: yyyy 年MM 月 dd 日
The standard time format is hh:mm:ss. The 24-hour clock format should be used
Time format conversions
Currency notation are placed before and after the numeral, with two decimal figures
Standard translations for specific expressions
rules.example_correct:
關於
如圖 xx 所示
如需關於 xxx 的更多資訊，請參閱 xxx
不可用
請注意：
如需關於 xxx 的說明，請參閱 xxx
註冊商標
疑難排解
商標
使用者指南
希望
建議您…
遵循這些步驟 xxx
按一下
按兩下
rules.notes: For about, as shown in figure xx, For more information on xxx see xxx, N/A, Please note that, Refer to xxx for instructions on xxx, registered trademark, troubleshooting, trademark, User's Guide, want (to) would like, we recommend, Follow these steps to xxx, Click, Double click

rules.description: Refer to the most updated version of the approved RingCentral term list for your language
rules.notes: RingCentral has a number of registered and unregistered trademarks and trademarked phrases

rules.description: Use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on the first mention in the translated version of your content
rules.notes: Unless otherwise indicated

rules.description: For third party products mentioned in RingCentral content, complete extensive research to determine whether an official, approved trademarked translated version is available
rules.notes: Otherwise, use as in source

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: Complete extensive research to determine whether an official, approved trademarked translated version is available before using as in source
rules.notes: For product names from third parties, follow the localized equivalence in official websites if there's one

rules.description: Make sure you receive instructions on whether an official and approved translated version is available
rules.notes: Copyright notices tend to be standard texts

rules.description: Query if URL links have a local version, and modify the document accordingly
rules.notes: If not, the relevant URL will be left as it appears in the source English text

rules.description: If software has been translated, match the relevant translation memory or string list provided with the translation package
rules.notes: Instructions may vary based on RingCentral project-specific guidelines

rules.description: If software has not been translated, use bilingual format, i.e. English first, followed by target in brackets
Refer to Microsoft approved terminology for your language on the MS Portal when necessary
rules.notes: http://www.microsoft.com/Language/en-US/Default.aspx

rules.description: Leave the address in English
If source only includes toll free numbers and US numbers, add an international country code to the local number for international users
rules.example_correct: RingCentral, Inc. (Belmont/HQ) 20 Davis Drive, Belmont, California 94402 電話：888-528-RING (7464) 或 +1-650-472-4100
rules.example_incorrect:
RingCentral, Inc. (Belmont/HQ) 20 Davis Drive, Belmont, California 94402
Phone: 888-528-RING (7464) or 650-472-4100
